attacking_style: Specializes in late, precisely-timed solo attacks inside the final 1-2km, particularly on punchy finishes after controlled climbs. Opportunistic against distracted or sprint-focused teams.
team_tactics: Operates with limited team support; relies on other teams (UAE, Jumbo) controlling climbs, then exploits positioning in final kilometer when attention shifts to GC battles or sprint preparation.
terrain_preference: Punchy climbs followed by short run-ins to finish; rolling, technical finales where positioning beats pure power. Jaizkibel-type ascents suit better than expected. Struggles less on power climbs than typical sprinters.
vulnerability: Requires other teams to control race and expend energy; lacks team firepower for sustained offensive. Sacrifices stage wins for secondary objectives (points classification) when odds unfavorable.
key_patterns: Final kilometer attacks when peloton distracted by GC/sprint dynamics (2 instances)
Tactical flexibility: switches from breakaway to sprint points accumulation mid-race
Punchy climb survival allows positioning advantage over pure sprinters (2+ instances)
Opportunistic racing against stronger teams when their focus elsewhere
form_trajectory: positive: breakthrough Tour stage win, matched Roglič on punchy climbs, showing improved climbing range and tactical maturity in 2023
